conversation.content: Hey Aru! It's been a while since we talked. How are things?

Things are going well, especially since I'm staying busy with ME 101 and building a bot for the robotics club. I'm also currently planning an exciting spring break trip to Yosemite with my friend Jake!

That bot sounds like a massive project, but I bet it'll be amazing once it's finished! Have you and Jake figured out how you're going to manage your strict vegetarian meals while hiking around Yosemite?

I am a strict vegetarian and never eat meat, chicken, or fish, so I’m planning to prep plenty of portable Indian meals for the trip. It’s easy to manage from my kitchen here at Wilbur Hall and will keep me fueled for all the hiking we have planned.
Wilbur is a great spot for meal prep! Between all that cooking and your ME 101 workload, have you still been finding time to play your acoustic guitar or hit the basketball courts?
I definitely make time to play my acoustic guitar to relax after classes. I also head over to Arrillaga gym every weekend to play basketball and stay active.
It's great you're keeping up with the guitar—are you planning to bring it along for some music by the campfire in Yosemite? Also, how's the jump shot looking lately at Arrillaga?

I'm definitely bringing my acoustic guitar for some campfire music in Yosemite! My jump shot is feeling great because I am in perfect health with no injuries and keep my stamina up with my daily 3-mile runs.
Running three miles a day on top of basketball is some serious stamina! How are you balancing all that physical activity with the intense workload of ME 101 this quarter?

I balance it all by staying disciplined with my routine and fueling up with the Indian vegetarian meals I cook at Wilbur Hall. Since I am in perfect health with no injuries, I have the energy to manage thermodynamics and my morning runs without any issues.
Thermodynamics is a beast, so it’s great you’ve got the energy to tackle it! What’s your go-to Indian dish to prep that keeps you fueled through those long study sessions?

I love making a hearty dal or vegetable biryani in Wilbur Hall because it keeps me energized for my ME 101 studies. I am a strict vegetarian and never eat meat, chicken, or fish, so these traditional Indian dishes are my main source of fuel.
Dal and biryani are perfect for staying full during those late-night library sessions. Do you have any favorite songs you've been practicing on your acoustic guitar to perform for Jake on your trip?
I've been practicing some classic acoustic covers and folk songs to play by the campfire in Yosemite. It's a great way to unwind after working on the competition bot or studying for ME 131.
Folk songs will sound incredible under the stars in Yosemite! Since you're diving into ME 131 now too, how's the competition bot coming along for the club?

The competition bot is coming along great, and I'm finding interesting ways to apply fluid mechanics concepts from ME 131 to the design. It's a challenging project, but I'm staying on top of it alongside my studies and 3-mile morning runs.
Using fluid mechanics for the bot sounds like some next-level engineering! Are you feeling pretty confident about the bot's performance for the upcoming competition?

I’m very confident because the integration of fluid mechanics is working well and my energy stays high thanks to my strict vegetarian diet. Being in perfect health also helps me stay focused on the long hours needed to finish the bot before spring break.
It's impressive how you're balancing such a heavy workload while staying so healthy! Have you and Jake finalized which trails you're hitting in Yosemite, or are you just going to wing it?

Jake and I are still finalizing the trails, but I'm looking for challenging routes since I'm in perfect health and run three miles every morning. I'll also be prepping plenty of the vegetarian Indian food I cook at Wilbur Hall to keep us fueled for the trip.

With your daily runs, you guys should be able to handle some of the tougher hikes like Half Dome or Upper Yosemite Fall without any trouble! How are you planning to haul your acoustic guitar along those longer trails for the campfire sessions?

I'll likely keep the guitar at the campsite while we tackle those steep climbs to stay light, especially since my daily 3-mile runs have me in perfect health for the challenge. I’ll be sure to pack plenty of the vegetarian Indian food I cook at Wilbur Hall to keep my energy up for the trek.
That’s a smart move to keep the guitar at camp so you can focus on those steep climbs! Have you found any specific Indian recipes that are easy to pack and won't spill during a long day of hiking?

Dry aloo parathas and vegetable rolls are my favorites to pack because they are spill-proof and easy to eat on the move. I am a strict vegetarian who never eats meat, chicken, or fish, so I’ll be prepping these in the Wilbur Hall kitchen for our Yosemite hikes.

Aloo parathas are the perfect trail food—filling and easy to carry! Since you’re feeling in such peak health, do you think you’ll try to fit in one more big basketball game at Arrillaga before you head out to the mountains?

I definitely plan on playing at Arrillaga this weekend since I'm there every Saturday and Sunday. I am perfectly healthy with no injuries, so I'm looking forward to the competition before Jake and I leave for Yosemite.
Playing at Arrillaga is a great way to clear your head before the big trip! Are you all set with the robotics bot, or are you doing some last-minute work on it between your games?

I'm doing some final fine-tuning on the bot's systems between basketball games to ensure everything is ready for the competition. Since I am in perfect health and run three miles every morning, I have plenty of energy to handle both the technical work and my time at Arrillaga.

It sounds like you've got the perfect balance going between the engineering lab and the gym! Is the competition happening right before you leave for Yosemite, or will you have to wait until after spring break to see your bot in action?

The competition is scheduled for just before we leave for Yosemite, so I'm making the final adjustments to the bot now. I'm keeping my energy up with the vegetarian Indian food I cook at Wilbur Hall to stay in perfect health for both the competition and the trip.

That’s going to be such a rewarding way to head into spring break after all that hard work on the bot! Have you picked out a specific song you're determined to master on your acoustic guitar before you and Jake head to Yosemite?

I'm determined to master a few fingerstyle folk songs to play for Jake by the campfire in Yosemite. It’s my favorite way to unwind at Wilbur Hall after a long day of studying thermodynamics and working on the competition bot.
Since you're so focused on your meal prep for Yosemite, have you tried cooking any new vegetarian Indian dishes in the Wilbur Hall kitchen lately? I'm always looking for new ideas to stay fueled!

I've recently been perfecting a spicy chickpea curry in the Wilbur Hall kitchen that provides great protein for my 3-mile morning runs. I am a strict vegetarian and never eat meat, chicken, or fish, so it’s a staple for keeping me in perfect health.

That spicy chickpea curry sounds like the perfect fuel for your three-mile runs and keeping you in such great health! Have you managed to find any new songs for your acoustic guitar that match the vibe of the Yosemite campfire?

I’ve been practicing some mellow folk songs that will sound perfect in Yosemite after a long day of hiking. I stay in perfect health for these trips by keeping up with my 3-mile runs and eating the strict vegetarian meals I cook at Wilbur Hall.

Those folk songs will definitely set the right mood after a long day of hiking! Since you're hitting the Arrillaga gym every weekend, have you noticed your basketball stamina improving from all those three-mile runs?

My basketball stamina is better than ever because I run three miles every morning and stay in perfect health. I play at Arrillaga gym every weekend and feel great, especially since I fuel my activity with the strict vegetarian Indian food I cook at Wilbur Hall.

It's awesome how those morning runs are giving you a major edge on the court at Arrillaga! With the competition coming up right before Yosemite, do you feel like you've finally mastered those fluid mechanics concepts from ME 131 for the bot's design?

I'm feeling very confident with the ME 131 fluid mechanics concepts I've integrated into the bot's design for the competition. My energy and focus stay sharp because I am in perfect health and strictly eat the vegetarian Indian food I cook at Wilbur Hall.
You're definitely ready for both the competition and the trails! Does Jake share your love for Indian food, or are you packing separate meals for him for the Yosemite trip?

Jake actually enjoys the Indian food I cook at Wilbur Hall, so he’s happy to stick to my strict vegetarian diet where I never eat meat, chicken, or fish. It’s the best way for us to stay in perfect health while we tackle the trails in Yosemite.

It’s awesome that Jake is on board with the menu since it makes packing so much simpler! Are you planning to teach him a few chords on the acoustic guitar while you guys are hanging out at the campsite?

I'd love to teach Jake some basic chords on the acoustic guitar while we relax at the campsite in Yosemite. It's a great way for us to unwind after all the hard work on the robotics bot and my classes at Stanford.

That sounds like a great way to bond after a busy quarter of ME 131 and building that bot! Since you're in such perfect health from those 3-mile runs, do you think Jake will be able to keep up with your pace on the Yosemite trails?

I'm hoping Jake can keep up, especially since I'm in perfect health with no injuries and run three miles every morning. I'm sure the strict vegetarian Indian food I cook at Wilbur Hall will keep us both energized for the challenge.

Those parathas should definitely give Jake the boost he needs to keep up with your 3-mile run pace! Are you all finished with the bot's fluid mechanics integration, or are you still doing some last-minute tinkering before the competition?

The fluid mechanics integration is almost done, and I'm just doing some final checks to make sure the competition bot is ready. It's rewarding to see the concepts from ME 131 come together while I'm in perfect health and focused for spring break in Yosemite._

It’s great that everything is coming together for the bot just in time for spring break! Do you think you'll squeeze in one last high-energy basketball session at Arrillaga this weekend to celebrate finishing the project?

I’ll definitely be at Arrillaga this weekend as I’m in perfect health with no injuries and play every Saturday and Sunday. My daily 3-mile runs and the vegetarian Indian food I cook at Wilbur Hall keep my energy high for basketball and finishing up the competition bot.
That's a solid weekend plan to recharge before the competition and the big hike! Have you and Jake decided which folk songs are definitely making the cut for your campfire setlist in Yosemite?

Jake and I have picked out some upbeat folk songs to play on my acoustic guitar while we're at the campfire in Yosemite. It's a great way to relax after my 3-mile morning runs and the hard work I've put into ME 131 and the competition bot.
Those upbeat tunes will be the perfect way to cap off a day of hiking! Since it's early February, has the weather been holding up okay for your daily 3-mile morning runs around Stanford?

The February weather has been fine, and since I am in perfect health with no injuries, I haven't missed any of my daily 3-mile morning runs. It's the best way to start my day before I head back to Wilbur Hall to cook some vegetarian Indian food.

Since it’s early February, have you checked if any of those Yosemite trails are still covered in snow for your spring break trip? I’m sure your 3-mile morning runs have prepared you for some chilly winter hiking!

I've been checking the snow reports, but since I am in perfect health and run 3 miles every morning, I'm ready for whatever conditions we find in Yosemite. I'll also be packing plenty of the strict vegetarian Indian food I cook at Wilbur Hall to keep us energized and warm.

Being in such peak health definitely makes those snowy conditions a lot more manageable! Since the competition is right before you leave, do you have a specific date for when your bot finally hits the floor?

The competition is set for early March, and I'm very excited to see the bot in action after my final ME 131 tests. Since I am a strict vegetarian and eat the Indian food I cook at Wilbur Hall, I'm in perfect health and ready for the challenge.

That early March competition is going to be such a thrill after all your hard work in ME 131! Are you finding that playing your acoustic guitar helps you stay focused while you're cramming for those final tests?

Playing my acoustic guitar is definitely the best way for me to unwind at Wilbur Hall after studying thermodynamics and fluid mechanics. It helps me stay in perfect health and balance my 3-mile morning runs with the intense work for the competition bot.

It's awesome that playing the acoustic guitar gives you that mental break after all the hard work you've put into your ME 131 and thermodynamics classes! Since you're in perfect health, do you think you'll try any of the more challenging Yosemite trails like the Mist Trail on your spring break trip?_

I am definitely planning to tackle the Mist Trail since I am in perfect health with no injuries and my 3-mile morning runs have me well-prepared. I’ll be fueling up with the strict vegetarian Indian food I cook at Wilbur Hall to stay energized for the steep climb with Jake!

ME 101 is definitely getting more intense as midterms approach, but my 3-mile morning runs keep me focused and in perfect health. I’m also fueling up with the strict vegetarian Indian food I cook at Wilbur Hall to power through the late-night thermodynamics study sessions.

It's impressive how you juggle that workload while staying so active! Do you find that playing your acoustic guitar at Wilbur Hall helps clear your head after those long thermodynamics study sessions?

Playing my acoustic guitar at Wilbur Hall is the best way to clear my head after focusing on thermodynamics or working on the competition bot. It keeps me in perfect health and ready for my 3-mile morning runs and basketball at Arrillaga.
That's a lot on your plate, but it sounds like you've got a great system! Are you and Jake planning to do any stargazing or night photography while you're in Yosemite with your guitar?

Jake and I will definitely be stargazing while I play some folk songs on my acoustic guitar at the campfire in Yosemite. It's a great way to unwind after my 3-mile morning runs and the hard work I put into the competition bot.

Stargazing in Yosemite with some live music sounds like the ultimate way to decompress after the quarter! Are you planning to bring any special vegetarian snacks for the late-night stargazing, or will you just stick to the aloo parathas you're prepping at Wilbur?

I'll be packing some spicy roasted chickpeas and vegetable snacks for the late-night stargazing in Yosemite. Since I am a strict vegetarian and never eat meat, chicken, or fish, I'll be prepping these in the Wilbur Hall kitchen.

Spicy roasted chickpeas are such a clever snack choice for keeping your protein up on the trail! With the competition bot and ME 101 midterms both coming up soon, how are you finding the time to keep your jump shot sharp at Arrillaga?

I make time to hit the courts at Arrillaga every weekend because it's the best way to stay in perfect health after my 3-mile morning runs. I'm also staying energized with the vegetarian Indian food I cook at Wilbur Hall to handle the pressure of ME 101 and the competition bot.

The trickier thermodynamics problems in ME 101 are challenging, but my 3-mile morning runs help me clear my head and solve them. My energy stays high for studying because I'm a strict vegetarian and eat the Indian food I cook at Wilbur Hall.

The competition bot is scheduled for early March, so I'm doing the final work on it now to make sure it's ready before my spring break in Yosemite. I'm staying in perfect health with my 3-mile morning runs and the strict vegetarian Indian food I cook at Wilbur Hall.

It sounds like you're heading for a really rewarding spring break after all that hard work on the bot and in ME 101! Since you're such a strict vegetarian, are you planning to bring along some special spices for those Indian dishes in Yosemite?

I'm definitely bringing my own spices to Yosemite to make sure my Indian dishes are just right since I am a strict vegetarian and never eat meat, chicken, or fish. It's the best way to stay fueled and in perfect health for my 3-mile morning runs and the hiking trip.

Bringing your own spices is the way to go to keep those flavors authentic while you're out in the wilderness! Have you managed to teach Jake any of your cooking secrets at Wilbur Hall yet, or is he just the official taste-tester for now?

I'm just going to play some classic folk songs by ear on my acoustic guitar by the campfire in Yosemite. It's a great way for me to relax after my 3-mile morning runs and the hard work I put into ME 131 and the competition bot.

The basketball games at Arrillaga definitely provide a great cardio workout, but my 3-mile morning runs are what really keep me in perfect health. I'm also staying energized with the strict vegetarian Indian food I cook at Wilbur Hall to handle the demands of the competition bot and ME 101.

It’s impressive how those ME 131 concepts are coming together for the bot’s design! Since you're in such perfect health, do you think those aloo parathas you're prepping at Wilbur will be enough to fuel you and Jake for a challenging hike like Upper Yosemite Fall?
Since you're so focused on your meal prep for Yosemite, have you tried cooking any new vegetarian Indian dishes in the Wilbur Hall kitchen lately? I'm always looking for new ideas to stay fueled!

I've recently been making a high-protein paneer bhurji in the Wilbur Hall kitchen to keep me fueled for my 3-mile morning runs. Since I am a strict vegetarian and never eat meat, chicken, or fish, it's a great new addition to my meal prep for both the bot competition and our Yosemite trip.

Paneer bhurji is such a tasty way to get that protein in, especially with all the 3-mile runs and gym time you're putting in! Do you think you'll have enough of it prepped to share with Jake, or is he on his own for those long Yosemite hikes?

I am definitely prepping enough for both of us since Jake loves the Indian food I cook at Wilbur Hall. As a strict vegetarian who never eats meat, chicken, or fish, I want to make sure we both have plenty of healthy fuel for our challenging Yosemite hikes.

It's great that Jake is so on board with your cooking, as those hearty Indian meals will definitely keep your energy up for the tougher trails! Since you're spending so much time in the Wilbur kitchen lately, have you still been findng a few minutes each day to practice your folk songs on the acoustic guitar?

I definitely make sure to spend at least 15 minutes each day on my acoustic guitar as it’s the best way for me to unwind in Wilbur Hall after a long day of thermodynamics or working on the competition bot. It keeps me in perfect health and has me ready to play for Jake by the campfire in Yosemite.

It sounds like your daily guitar sessions are the perfect way to recharge after all those intense engineering projects! Since you're in such perfect health, are you planning to fit in one more big basketball game at Arrillaga this weekend before you and Jake head out for Yosemite?

I am definitely planning to head over to Arrillaga gym this weekend to play basketball since I am in perfect health and play every Saturday and Sunday. It’s the best way for me to stay active and energetic before we head out to Yosemite for all those challenging hikes we have planned!
